name: brainstorming
description: Use when creating or developing anything, before writing code or implementation plans - refines rough ideas into fully-formed designs through structured Socratic questioning, alternative exploration, and incremental validation
capabilities: Guides three-phase brainstorming process (Understanding, Exploration, Design)
Uses structured questioning to clarify vague requirements
Presents architectural alternatives with explicit trade-offs
Validates design incrementally in 200-300 word sections
Provides templates for decision matrices and design documents
author: ArieGoldkin
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown templates and examples. No executable code, scripts, network operations, or file access capabilities. All 132 static findings are false positives triggered by documentation patterns - the scanner detected terminology in markdown files (e.g., 'md5', 'backticks', 'network', 'cookies') without any actual vulnerable code. The skill provides prompting guidance only.
valueStatement: Ideas often start vague and incomplete. This skill guides you through a three-phase process to clarify requirements, explore alternatives, and validate designs incrementally before any code is written. Use this skill when creating or developing anything, before writing implementation plans.
userTitle: Refine ideas into designs through structured brainstorming
limitations: Does not write code or implementation plans
Does not access or modify project files
Does not execute commands or scripts
Requires user engagement for effective brainstorming

useCases.title: Clarify product requirements
useCases.description: Transform high-level feature ideas into clear, validated requirements before development begins

useCases.title: Explore architectural approaches
useCases.description: Evaluate technology choices with explicit trade-off analysis and decision documentation

useCases.title: Prevent costly rework
useCases.description: Catch design gaps early by validating assumptions before implementation starts

promptTemplates.title: Initial idea
promptTemplates.prompt: I have an idea for [feature/product]. Help me explore and design it.

promptTemplates.title: Architecture selection
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to build [system]. What are the best approaches and their trade-offs?

promptTemplates.title: Requirements clarification
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me understand what I actually need for [project]. Ask me questions to clarify.

promptTemplates.title: Design validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Here is my initial design for [feature]. Validate it section by section and identify gaps.

bestPractices: Announce skill usage at the start of brainstorming sessions
Ask one question at a time and wait for responses before proceeding
Always propose 2-3 approaches with explicit trade-off comparisons
Validate each design section before moving to the next
antiPatterns: Jumping to solutions before understanding the problem
Presenting only one approach without alternatives
Skipping incremental validation and presenting full designs at once
Forcing linear progression when new information requires revisiting earlier phases

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It uses the AskUserQuestion tool for structured choices.

faq.question: What types of projects benefit most?
faq.answer: Complex features, new systems, or any project where requirements are vague or multiple approaches could work.

faq.question: How does this integrate with existing workflows?
faq.answer: Use this before writing code or implementation plans. Document results using the included templates.

faq.question: Is any data stored or transmitted?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-based skill with no code execution, file access, or network operations.

faq.question: What if requirements change mid-session?
faq.answer: The skill supports non-linear progression. Return to earlier phases to incorporate new constraints.

faq.question: How is this different from general prompting?
faq.answer: It provides a structured three-phase framework with explicit tool usage guidelines and templates.
